title: SD-WAN Branch Not Failing Over to Backup Circuit

ticket.summary: Branch office BR-12 lost its primary MPLS circuit at 09:14. Users report a full 6-minute outage before traffic resumed on the backup broadband circuit, even though the SD-WAN policy is supposed to fail over within 5-10 seconds (BFD-based).

topology.description: BR-12's edge router has two WAN transports: WAN1 (MPLS, primary) and WAN2 (broadband, backup), both terminating control connections to the SD-WAN controller/orchestrator (vSmart) over the internet. A path is only usable once BOTH its data plane (BFD) and its control-plane connection to vSmart are up.

links.label: BFD up; control connection silently down since 08:55

logs.content:
09:14:02 BFD-6-SESSION_DOWN: neighbor 10.20.5.1 on WAN1 (MPLS) - session down, no packets received
09:14:02 SDWAN-5-POLICY: WAN1 marked DOWN, evaluating failover to WAN2
09:14:07 SDWAN-3-CONTROL_CONN: control connection to vSmart controller via WAN2 not yet established
09:20:11 SDWAN-6-CONTROL_CONN: control connection to vSmart controller via WAN2 established
09:20:12 SDWAN-5-POLICY: WAN2 promoted to active path

cli_outputs.output:
WAN1: DOWN since 09:14:02
WAN2: UP (data plane), control connection: DOWN until 09:20:11
WAN2 tloc: control connection flapped at 08:55, did not reconnect until 09:20:11

monitoring.notes: WAN2's control-plane (OMP/vSmart) connection had been silently down since 08:55, ~19 minutes before the MPLS failure, due to an earlier transient internet blip that never recovered. Data plane on WAN2 was reachable the whole time, but the fabric wouldn't promote a path with no control connection.

hints: BFD correctly detected the MPLS failure in seconds — the delay is on the WAN2 side, not WAN1.
Look at the control connection timestamps: WAN2's control connection to the vSmart controller was already down before the outage even started.

SD-WAN routers need both data-plane reachability AND an active control connection to a controller before that path is used to carry traffic; a broken control session on the backup circuit blocks failover even if data plane is fine.

solution.root_cause: The backup circuit (WAN2)'s control-plane connection to the vSmart controller had silently failed ~19 minutes before the MPLS outage and never re-established, so even though WAN2's data plane was healthy, the SD-WAN fabric would not promote it to active until the control connection recovered at 09:20:11.
solution.fix: Alert on control-connection state per-TLOC (not just BFD/data-plane state) so a silently-down control session on a backup circuit is caught and remediated before it's needed; in this case, restarting or investigating WAN2's control session proactively would have prevented the extended outage.
acceptable_answers: data plane up but control down
